tasks.description: Fix validateAndCoerceParams to pass through unrecognized params. In `src/tools/video-editor/effects/validateParams.ts` around line 19, change the `reduce` initializer from `{}` to `{ ...(params ?? {}) }` so extra params survive while schema-defined ones get validated/coerced on top. Then update the test in `DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx` around lines 275-287: add an unrecognized param (e.g. `customSeed: 42`) to the input and assert it passes through in the output alongside coerced schema params.

Fix register()/registerAsync() dedup to use structural schema comparison instead of reference equality. In `src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts`: (1) Add a private helper `schemasEqual(a?, b?)` that returns `a === b || JSON.stringify(a) === JSON.stringify(b)`. (2) Update line ~48 in `register()`: change `existing.schema === schema` to `this.schemasEqual(existing.schema, schema)`. (3) Update line ~58 in `registerAsync()` early-return: same change. (4) Update line ~62 in `registerAsync()` pending staleness check: change `pending.schema !== schema` to `!this.schemasEqual(pending.schema, schema)`. Then add a test in `DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx` near the existing dedup test (~line 50): register same effect with structurally-equal but referentially-distinct schema object and assert no extra notification fires; then register with a genuinely different schema and assert it does fire.

Guard replaceEffectRegistry call so it only writes when registry actually changes. In `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts` around line 29: (1) Add `getEffectRegistry` to the import from `@/tools/video-editor/effects` (already exported at `effects/index.tsx:82`). (2) Replace the bare `replaceEffectRegistry(registry)` call with `if (getEffectRegistry() !== registry) replaceEffectRegistry(registry);`. No test changes needed.

Skip inner Sequence offset when startFrame is 0 in EffectLayerSequence. In `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/EffectLayerSequence.tsx` around line 39: replace the unconditional `<Sequence from={-startFrame}>{children}</Sequence>` with a conditional: `const inner = startFrame === 0 ? children : <Sequence from={-startFrame}>{children}</Sequence>;` and pass `inner` to `wrapWithEffect` instead. No test changes needed — `<Sequence from={0}>` is a Remotion no-op.

Run all tests and verify fixes. (1) Run `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx` to confirm unit tests pass. (2) Run `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/` for broader video-editor coverage. (3) Write a short throwaway script that exercises each bug scenario (pass-through params, structural schema dedup, registry guard idempotency, startFrame=0 Sequence skip), run it, confirm correct behavior, then delete the script. If any test fails, read the error, fix the code, and re-run until green.

watch_items: DEBT: video-editor-effect-registry: replaceEffectRegistry() in render mutates module state during React render — T3's guard mitigates but does not fully resolve the render-purity concern (FLAG-002). Do not make this worse.
DEBT: video-editor-effect-registry: O(n) re-renders during effect initialization — these fixes should not increase notification frequency. Verify structural dedup doesn't regress this.

DEBT: clip-param-schema-evolution: clips with stale params from previous schema version are not auto-normalized. T1's pass-through fix preserves unrecognized params which may include stale ones — this is intentional per plan assumptions but keep in mind.
Assumption: ParameterSchema arrays are plain JSON-serializable data, so JSON.stringify comparison is reliable. If schemas contain functions or circular refs, this will break silently.

Assumption: Extra non-schema params in validateAndCoerceParams are intentional and used by effects, not stale junk. The pass-through change is a one-way door — once effects depend on it, removing it would be breaking.

T2 structural comparison: JSON.stringify is order-sensitive. If schema arrays can have keys in different order across serialization boundaries, dedup may still fail. Verify the actual schema construction paths produce consistent key ordering.
T4: Remotion Sequence with from={0} is assumed to be a pure no-op. Verify no Remotion internals rely on Sequence wrapper presence for context/timing.

sense_checks.question: Does the reduce initializer `{ ...(params ?? {}) }` correctly allow schema-defined params to overwrite their spread copies during reduce iteration, so coercion still takes effect for known params?

After adding schemasEqual, do all three call sites (register line ~48, registerAsync line ~58, registerAsync line ~62) use the helper consistently, and does the test cover both the no-fire (structural equal) and fire (genuinely different) cases?

Is getEffectRegistry already exported from `@/tools/video-editor/effects` (check effects/index.tsx), and does the guard `getEffectRegistry() !== registry` use reference equality (which is correct since registry is a useMemo singleton)?
Does the `inner` variable get passed to `wrapWithEffect` correctly, and is there no other code path in EffectLayerSequence that depends on the Sequence wrapper being present unconditionally?
Do all existing tests in DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx pass, including the new structural dedup and pass-through param tests, with no regressions in the broader video-editor test suite?

meta_commentary: Four independent, small fixes — each is 1-5 lines of production code plus a test tweak. Execute T1-T4 in parallel since they touch different files with no dependencies. Key judgment calls: (1) T2's JSON.stringify for schema comparison is pragmatic but order-sensitive — the plan assumes schemas are constructed consistently, which is true for the current EffectCreatorPanel path. (2) T3's render-time guard is acknowledged as a pragmatic fix per FLAG-002 — a full useEffect migration is out of scope and would break child synchronous visibility. (3) T1's spread initializer means the reduce overwrites spread values for schema-defined keys, which is correct since reduce runs after the spread. Watch for the existing git working tree having uncommitted changes in several of these files — check git status before starting and work with the current state of the files, not assumptions from the plan's line numbers.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Pass through unrecognized params in validateAndCoerceParams
Fix dedup in register()/registerAsync() with structural schema comparison
Guard replaceEffectRegistry call with identity check
Skip inner Sequence offset when startFrame is 0
Run tests to verify all fixes

validation.completeness_notes: All 5 plan steps mapped 1:1 to tasks. No gaps or deviations.
